text: The registry tries to unify too many concerns (deploy, monitoring, systemd, docs) into one schema with many optional/null fields
The node -e helper interpolates shell values into JS source, omits --input-type=commonjs, has no error handling
Authority split between services.json and conventions.md creates a third file to check, not fewer
Validation via Heimdall is aspirational — listed as unknown in same draft that proposes it
A TSV/bash manifest or direct fix of drift points may be sufficient for 7 services
The core entity definition excludes fortnox-mcp which is a real current consumer of security-scan.sh — forces a modeling decision about scope
Generator validation is single-host but services span two hosts — cross-host validation not addressed
Making registry truly authoritative requires updating architecture.md and authority.md, not just conventions.md port table
